items.title: Trunk-based development
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  M1((main A)) --> M2((main B)) --> M3((main C)) --> M4((main D))
  M2 -.short branch.-> F1((feat1)) -.merge same day.-> M3
  M3 -.short branch.-> F2((feat2)) -.merge same day.-> M4
  Tag1["tag v1.2.0"] -.on.-> M2
  Tag2["tag v1.3.0"] -.on.-> M4
items.caption: Trunk-based: tiny branches, daily merges to `main`, releases are tags on `main`.

items.title: GitFlow (classic, heavyweight)
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  D1((develop)) --> D2((develop)) --> D3((develop)) --> D4((develop))
  D1 --> F1((feature/x)) --> D2
  D2 --> F2((feature/y)) --> D3
  D3 --> R1((release/1.0)) --> M1((main tag v1.0))
  M1 --> H1((hotfix/login)) --> M2((main tag v1.0.1))
  H1 -.also merge back.-> D4
items.caption: GitFlow keeps `develop` as the integration branch, cuts `release/*` to stabilize, and `hotfix/*` from `main` to patch production.
